title: CATCH: Context-based Meta Reinforcement Learning for Transferrable Architecture Search
authors: Not specified in the provided text
abstract: CATCH is a novel context-guided meta reinforcement learning framework for neural architecture search that is both search space-agnostic and swiftly adaptive to new tasks. The framework addresses the limitations of current NAS methods that lack mechanisms to seek and preserve information for reuse across tasks.

methodology.name: CATCH (Context-based Meta Reinforcement Learning)

components.name: Context Encoder
components.description: Encodes task properties into latent context variables using amortized variational inference

components.name: RL Controller
components.description: Makes sequential decisions to generate candidate networks in a stochastic manner

methodology.task_definition: Problems that share the same action space but differ in reward functions. In NAS, the change of either the dataset or domain (e.g. from classification to detection) alters the underlying reward function, and thus can be treated as different tasks.
methodology.figure_reference: Figure 1: Shows the overall framework of CATCH with its three core components

problem_addressed: Current NAS methods lack mechanisms to seek and preserve information that can be meaningfully reused
Inefficient repeated search from scratch for new tasks
Need for solutions that can efficiently handle multiple tasks
Need for solutions that are widely applicable to different search spaces
Need for solutions that maintain competency across various settings
related_work: Works searching for architectures that perform well across tasks [23, 36]
